merger_description:
Enverus, Inc. (**Enverus**), a Texas corporation, proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of SBS Optionco, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and SBS Blocker, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (collectively, with each of their direct and indirect subsidiaries, **Spatial Business Systems**).

Enverus is a global Software-as-a-Service and data provider to the energy industry. Founded in 1999, and headquartered in Austin, Texas, Enverus offers a platform that integrates analytics, supply chain management, and commercial intelligence tools for oil and gas, power, utilities and renewable energy customers. Enverus is controlled by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc.

Spatial Business Systems is a global business headquartered in Littleton, Colorado in the United States. Spatial Business Systems supplies design software for utilities and critical network infrastructure assets to customers across electric and gas utilities, engineering and construction firms and broadband infrastructure operators. Spatial Business Systems’ software solutions are designed to automate and streamline engineering and design workflows, integrate spatial and design data and improve design effectiveness.

In Australia, Spatial Business Systems provides distribution design and substation services to customers.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Enverus, Inc. (Enverus) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of SBS
Optionco, LLC and SBS Blocker, LLC (collectively, with each of their
direct and indirect subsidiaries, Spatial Business Systems), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Enverus, is a global Software-as-a-Service and data
provider to the energy industry. Enverus was founded in 1999 and is
headquartered in Austin, Texas. Enverus offers a platform that
integrates analytics, supply chain management, and commercial
intelligence tools for oil and gas, power, utilities and renewable
energy customers.
Enverus is controlled by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates
of Blackstone, Inc. (together with its affiliates or affiliated investment
funds, Blackstone). Blackstone, Inc. is a US-based corporation,
incorporated under the laws of Delaware, United States.
The target, Spatial Business Systems, is a global business
headquartered in Littleton, Colorado in the United States. Spatial
Business Systems supplies design software for utilities and critical
network infrastructure assets to customers across electric and gas
utilities, engineering and construction firms, and broadband
infrastructure operators. Spatial Business Systems’ software
solutions are designed to automate and streamline engineering and
design workflows, integrate spatial and design data, and improve
design effectiveness. In Australia, Spatial Business Systems
provides distribution design and substation design services to
customers.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap, and limited potential vertical
relationships, between Enverus or Blackstone’s controlled
portfolio companies and Spatial Business Systems in the
supply of software for designing electric and gas distribution
networks and substations to customers in Australia
b. Spatial Business Systems has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
